description_long: This object consists of two tall, upright flowering plants with lush, dark green to brownish lanceolate leaves densely arranged along the stems. Each plant features a cluster of vibrant orange-red flowers at the top, with petals that are broad, slightly ruffled, and arranged in a somewhat irregular, bilateral pattern. The stems are sturdy and vertical, supporting the weight of the foliage and the prominent flower clusters. The leaves are large, smooth-edged, and overlap each other, creating a dense, bushy appearance. The flowers are positioned terminally, crowning the top of each stem and standing out vividly against the darker foliage. The overall form is naturalistic and would be well-suited for placement in urban parks, gardens, or landscaped sidewalks.
description: A pair of tall, upright flowering plants with dense, dark green lanceolate leaves and striking orange-red flower clusters at the top. The flowers are broad-petaled and terminal, contrasting with the lush foliage.
description_view_0: Two upright plants with dense, overlapping leaves and prominent orange-red flowers clearly visible at the top.
description_view_1: Side view showing the vertical alignment of the stems and the layering of large, lanceolate leaves, with flowers partially visible.
description_view_2: Frontal view highlighting both plants, their dense foliage, and the full display of the terminal flower clusters.
description_view_3: Side-rear view emphasizing the elongated stems, the back of the leaves, and the flowers peeking from the top.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a bilateral configuration, with some overlapping and slight ruffling, forming an irregular but symmetrical cluster at the terminal end of the stem.
